stories.title: Leeds United must be brave with record fee to seize rare James Trafford chance
stories.summary: For the second summer in succession, James Trafford has snubbed Newcastle United and, instead, is set to join Leeds United. Assuming the Yorkshire club recognise the opportunity before them. Right now, talks are on going between Leeds and Manchester City over the fee required to take the England goalkeeper over the Pennines. Leeds’ focus cannot be the cost – it must be the benefit. Because in Trafford, the Whites have the opportunity to lock in a No. 1 for seasons to come. Even if that doesn’t transpire, most likely, it will be because they have secured a hefty profit on whatever they invest in the 23-year-old.

stories.title: Aughinish Alumina investigation makes ‘no adverse findings’ against Co Limerick plant
stories.summary: An investigation into Aughinish Alumina and the company’s export of raw material to Russia has made “no adverse findings” against the Co Limerick plant, Minister for Enterprise Peter Burke has said. In a statement setting out the findings of the inquiry, Burke said there was “no information of a sufficient evidentiary level” found to confirm that alumina produced by the Russian-owned plant was later being used to supply Russian arms manufacturers making missiles and weapons for Moscow’s war in Ukraine.

stories.title: Italy pursue Pep Guardiola for national team job after Carlo Ancelotti rejection | Football News
stories.summary: The Italian football federation (FIGC) has held talks with Pep Guardiola and Carlo Ancelotti regarding the national team manager position, confirmed newly appointed technical director Paolo Maldini. Speaking at a news conference on Thursday, the former Azzurri defender and captain confirmed the federation’s ambitious shortlist. “Honestly, today we can't give news about what's happening,” Maldini told reporters when asked specifically about Guardiola. But he did confirm that the FIGC had also approached legendary Italian manager Ancelotti, currently in charge of the Brazil national team. “We can't hide that we also talked with Carlo Ancelotti before talking with Pep.

stories.title: 'Political will' can protect Aughinish jobs - Ukraine MP
stories.summary: 'Real political will' can protect Aughinish Alumina jobs, Ukrainian MP says Updated / Friday, 24 Jul 2026 07:44 Getty Ivanna Klympush-Tsintsadze is chair of the committee on Ukraine's integration with the EU The chair of the committee on Ukraine's integration with the EU has said there is a way to protect jobs at Aughinish Alumina if the company is sanctioned should the Irish Government have "the political will" to do so. An independent review into the Co Limerick refinery made no "adverse findings", however, Minister for Enterprise Peter Burke cited a "lack of verifiable internal trade data within Russia" since the country's invasion of Ukraine.

stories.title: Alejandro Garnacho makes Man Utd admission as he sets Aston Villa objective after Chelsea transfer
stories.summary: New Aston Villa signing Alejandro Garnacho believes Unai Emery can help him rediscover the form that first propelled him into the limelight at Manchester United. The 22-year-old winger has joined Villa on an initial season-long loan , with the move set to become permanent if he makes a specified number of appearances next season. Garnacho only joined Chelsea last summer in a £40 million move from Manchester United, but he struggled to produce his best form during his time at Stamford Bridge. Now embarking on a new chapter in his career, the Argentina international is eager to get started under Emery and make a fresh impact. “I’m really happy to be here,” he said on Villa TV.

stories.title: Southport killer Rudakubana transferred to Broadmoor hospital in Berkshire
stories.summary: Southport killer Axel Rudakubana has been transferred from prison to Broadmoor psychiatric hospital, the Ministry of Justice has confirmed. Broadmoor, a high-security psychiatric facility, is located in Crowthorne, just south of Bracknell. Officials approved Rudakubana's transfer on behalf of ministers, but a spokesperson for the department stressed it does not alter his 52-year sentence. Next Wednesday (July 29) marks two years since Rudakubana carried out an attack at a Taylor Swift-themed dance class in summer 2024, killing three girls. The move to Broadmoor high security hospital from the category A prison Belmarsh was first reported by the Guardian.

stories.title: Russian Su-57 fighter jet crashes in Moscow Oblast – photos
stories.summary: A Russian Su-57 fighter jet has crashed in Moscow Oblast. Source: Telegram channel Exilenova+, which publishes information on Russian equipment losses citing sources among Russian forces Quote: "More footage from the Odintsovo district, Moscow Oblast, where the Su-57 crashed. As for the pilot – we'll wait for official confirmation. There's none yet. " Details: Exilenova+ had earlier reported that, according to Russian sources, the pilot had managed to eject. "Update: the 'unrivalled' Su-57 was shot down by Moscow's air defence," Exilenova+ later added. The Telegram channel Taras Hryhorovych Reports also confirmed the aircraft's downing, specifying that it occurred during a training flight.

stories.title: The Game Takes a Bottle to the Head at 'VERZUZ' With YG
stories.summary: The Game temporarily turned a concert into a drinking contest during his VERZUZ event with YG , downing almost an entire bottle of tequila in a single gulp. During the event on Thursday (July 23), the Compton (or, as he repeatedly said it throughout the evening, “Bompton”) rapper took a giant swallow of what looked like Don Julio while onstage, and dropped the empty bottle to the ground in celebration when he was done. Don’t believe us? See it for yourself below. This VERZUZ matchup pits two generations of Compton against each other. The Game, born Jayceon Terrell Taylor, broke out as a Dr.

stories.title: Miquita Oliver apologises to Fearne Cotton over past jibe
stories.summary: Miquita Oliver apologises to Fearne Cotton over 'awful' comment in the past Updated / Wednesday, 22 Jul 2026 18:15 Getty (L-R) Miquita Oliver and Fearne Cotton - Oliver was let go from her radio job in 2005 following an interview with NME where she was asked how she would improve Top of the Pops and replied that the programme should get rid of Cotton Presenter Miquita Oliver has apologised to Fearne Cotton after Oliver was sacked from BBC Radio 1 in 2005 for making an "awful" comment about her fellow broadcaster.

stories.title: Trial opens for US mum who says postpartum issues led her to killing own children
stories.summary: Warning: This story contains distressing details and discussion of attempted suicide Jury selection has begun in Massachusetts in the case of a woman charged with killing all three of her children - whose defence team say she was suffering from postpartum psychosis. Prosecutors allege the killings carried out by Lindsay Clancy at her home in 2023 were premeditated and intentional. She has pleaded not guilty. Her legal team does not dispute that she carried out the killings, but is expected to argue that she was not responsible and raise an insanity defence. Clancy herself was also paralysed after attempting to take her own life the same night.

stories.title: Imperious Hegarty named The Sunday Game Hurler of Year
stories.summary: The Sunday Game have chosen Gearoid Hegarty as their Hurler of the Year with dominant All-Ireland champions Limerick taking up no less than 10 positions on the show's Team of the Year. Hegarty was at his imperious best as the Treaty County romped past Galway in Sunday's showpiece at Croke Park and he forms one-third of an all-Limerick half-forward line in our best 15 of the 2026 season. Beaten finalists Galway take up three spots on the final selection with one each for Clare and Cork. The Sunday Game Team of the Year 1. Nickie Quaid (Limerick) Another banner year for Limerick's last line of defence.

stories.title: ‘Up in smoke’: Attacks on ‘Russia’s Amazon’ bring Putin’s war closer to home
stories.summary: A series of Ukrainian strikes on the warehouses of Russia’s largest online retailer has sent potentially hundreds of millions of dollars in inventory up in flames, sparking an online furor among the country’s embattled small-business owners. Over the weekend, Ukrainian drones hit Wildberries distribution centers in Elektrostal in the Moscow region and Kotovsk in the Tambov region. A second wave of attacks early Wednesday hit logistics warehouses in the Krasnodar and Stavropol regions, while state media in the southwestern Voronezh region also reported that a drone strike forced the closure of a Wildberries warehouse there late Wednesday into Thursday.

stories.title: Wildfire emergency: France requests EU support as 23,000 evacuated in Biscarrosse wildfire crisis
stories.summary: Firefighting aircraft and helicopters from Portugal, Croatia, Czech Republic, and Slovakia are expected to arrive soon to aid French emergency crews. France has formally asked for assistance from other European Union countries as it battles devastating wildfires in the southwest of the country. President Emmanuel Macron confirmed that France has activated the European Union Civil Protection Mechanism, seeking urgent support, particularly for aerial firefighting. Aircraft and helicopters from Portugal, Croatia, the Czech Republic, and Slovakia are expected to join the efforts in the coming days. https://x. com/EmmanuelMacron/status/2080421021006405681?

stories.title: Chandrashekhar Azad: ‘This protest should have been Opposition’s job, not of students’
stories.summary: Azad Samaj Party (Kanshi Ram) chief and Nagina MP Chandrashekhar Azad was among the first politicians to engage with the Cockroach Janata Party (CJP)-led youth protests at Delhi’s Jantar Mantar. Azad speaks to The Indian Express on the reasons behind the protests, the July 20 violence, the government’s response, and the initial reluctance of Opposition parties to engage with it. Excerpts: * You were among the first politicians to associate with the CJP-led protests. Why did you think it was important when few other Opposition parties openly supported it?

stories.title: Minister declines to say whether Burnham will heed Lineker’s call for wealth tax
stories.summary: A Treasury minister has declined to say whether Andy Burnham’s Government will heed a call from millionaires including Gary Lineker for a higher tax on their wealth. Chief Secretary to the Treasury Emma Reynolds welcomed the offer, but said any announcement on tax policy would be made in the budget. Sports pundit and former footballer Lineker is among 120 UK-based millionaires to sign an open letter to the Prime Minister saying they should pay more tax. The “Proud to Pay” letter published on Thursday claims the money raised from higher taxes on wealth could be used to reduce inequality, support public infrastructure, and back small businesses.

stories.title: UK HMRC Plans 30,000 Retail Enforcement Actions as Illegal Tobacco and Vape Sellers Face Tougher Crackdown
stories.summary: The UK government has announced that HM Revenue & Customs (HMRC) will carry out more than 30,000 interventions targeting businesses and retail premises during the 2026-2027 financial year. The actions will focus on tax fraud, illegal goods sales and businesses involved in unlawful activities through retail channels. The government said illegal tobacco and illegal vape sales remain areas of concern. The move shows that UK enforcement against illegal nicotine products is expanding from import and supply channels toward retail-level oversight, alongside the upcoming introduction of the Vaping Products Duty. Key Points HMRC plans more than 30,000 retail interventions in 2026-2027.

stories.title: The transfer market, coaching staff, and management are targeting a 22-man squad. Bologna also includes Amondarain. Freuler's departure opens another door.
stories.summary: The transfer market, coaching staff, and management are targeting a 22-man squad. Bologna also includes Amondarain. Freuler's departure opens another door. Change of plans. "I love small squads, where everyone can compete for a spot and be a key player. Without Europe, we don't need too much rotation. I think a squad of 18-20 players will be enough," Domenico Tedesco stated on the day of his presentation. Meanwhile, the manager can be pleased with the arrival of Mikel Amondarain, announced last night, who was one of the main midfield targets. Ten million plus two million in bonuses would snatch the player from English clubs who had been pursuing him for some time.

stories.title: Zapatero Insists on Innocence Amid Scrutiny Over Jewelry Origins
stories.summary: Former Prime Minister José Luis Rodríguez Zapatero insists on his innocence, stating he received the €1.3 million jewelry as a long-standing courtesy gift with no personal financial motive, reigniting discussions about political responsibility and transparency in Spain.

stories.title: Morning newspapers can already see Míchel’s hand in winning Ajax
stories.summary: Pro Shots Jul 23, 2026 22:55-07:00 Translated by Ajax drew praise after their 1-4 win over FK Vojvodina in the second qualifying round of the Conference League. A day later, the Dutch newspapers agreed that coach Míchel's influence is becoming increasingly visible. According to De Telegraaf , Ajax played for an hour exactly as the Spanish coach wanted. There are, however, still areas to improve. ''Attacking, at times flamboyant and technically polished. The fact that Ajax also missed a great many chances and faded badly in the final half-hour confirmed his words just before the match. '' Míchel had said it would take time before the players fully took on board his way of playing.

stories.title: Maya 2027.2 trains MotionMaker
stories.summary: Table of Contents Show For those who don’t know the tool: Autodesk Maya is a DCC for modeling, rigging, animation, FX, USD and rendering. MotionMaker sits in the Animation Editor, feeding previs, crowds and character blocking before rigs, layers, retargeting and Arnold renders take over. Maya gets the AI work where artists live The current Maya update is more interesting as a DCC update than as another AI press-cycle snack. The centre of gravity is MotionMaker, the AI-assisted animation system now moving from supplied motion models toward user-trained character models.

stories.title: Woman who lost husband to suicide after he was admitted to CUH says change is needed
stories.summary: Nancy Reilly said they were “crazy happy” but their idyllic life was shattered when Mark took his own life after being admitted to a single room in .

stories.title: Walla Walla Iris Society Annual Rhizome Sale
stories.summary: WALLA - Rhonda and Charles Nosler of Waitsburg have been members of the Walla Iris Society since 2019 and currently have over 150 iris varieties. The WWIS will hold its annual sale at the Milton-Freewater Community Building on August 1 and 2 from 9 a. m. to 5 p. m. Irises will be presented by name, with variety information and a photo. For newcomers to growing irises, planting and care instructions will be available. The sale will include tall, intermediate, border, miniature tall, and standard dwarf varieties of bearded irises. Bearded irises are perennials that grow well in the Walla Valley, with its hot summers, cold winters, and well-drained soil.

stories.title: One year, 17 towns, dozens of voices: what we heard along the coast of England and Wales
stories.summary: I f you were asked to summon up images of the coastline of England and Wales, you might think of sunny days, buckets, spades and carefree holidays. But for many young people growing up by the sea, decades of evidence tell a very different story. For them, rather than a picture-perfect horizon full of hope, the sea has become a boundary that swallows up opportunity. With a 73% decline in funding for youth services in England over the past 12 years, and a lack of opportunities that has led to nearly 1 million 16- to 24-year-oldsnot in work, education or training (Neet), the situation for young people, especially in places of deprivation, has never seemed more desperate.

stories.title: ‘A small snippet’: what pubs make of Burnham’s £100m rates giveaway
stories.summary: Landlords say high costs of running hospitality business outweigh potential savings of business rates discount One of Andy Burnham’s first policy measures as prime minister is a 20% reduction in business rates for pubs, clubs and live music venues. The policy, announced on Thursday, is part of a broader agenda to support the high street and will cost £100m. Continue reading. One of Andy Burnham’s first policy measures as prime minister is a 20% reduction in business rates for pubs, clubs and live music venues. So how does the policy measure up in the context of running a business and where does the money on your bar bill actually go?

stories.title: Peter Phillips and Harriet Sperling are joined by her teenage daughter, Georgina, to support Prince William at a charity polo day
stories.summary: From their first meeting to their wedding day, family has been at the heart of Peter Phillips and Harriet Sperling's royal romance. So it is of no surprise their three daughters continue to play such an important part in their lives now that they're married. Last week, Harriet and Peter were joined by her daughter, Georgina, as they made a low-key appearance at the DMMI Royal Charity Polo Cup at Guards Polo Club, Windsor.

stories.title: What is China’s Kimi K3 and why is the US so rattled by it?
stories.summary: What is China’s Kimi K3 and why is the US so rattled by it? Remember DeepSeek? The Chinese artificial intelligence startup that rattled the US tech industry with its model that nearly matched leading rivals, trained at a fraction of the cost? China may have done it again. The latest Chinese AI model making waves was developed by a lesser-known startup called Moonshot AI. In just two days after launch, Moonshot suspended new subscriptions after demand overwhelmed its computing capacity.

stories.title: US hits dozens of trading partners with new wave of tariffs
stories.summary: Imposed citing forced labour concerns, the levies replace a temporary global duty brought in after the US Supreme Court struck down tariffs in February. The US has imposed new tariffs on 60 trading partners as a temporary global levy brought in after the Supreme Court ruled against the Trump administration in February expires. The duties, ranging from 10% to 12. 5% and accounting for the vast majority of American imports, target key economic partners over claims they have failed to properly tackle forced labour – including the UK, China, the European Union, Canada, Japan and India.

stories.title: Socceroos star allegedly tests positive for cocaine after speeding
stories.summary: Socceroos World Cup star Cristian Volpato allegedly tests positive for cocaine after being twice caught for speeding in Sydney. Socceroos World Cup star Cristian Volpato has allegedly tested positive for cocaine after police twice stopped him for speeding in Sydney on the same night. The 22-year-old Australia midfielder was stopped in a BMW Coupe on the Anzac Bridge at 11:05pm local time on Thursday night after he was caught travelling between 86-94km/h in a 60km/h zone, police said. He was subjected to a roadside breath test, which returned a negative result.

stories.title: India's 'cockroach' protesters and government to hold talks as stalemate continues
stories.summary: The meeting comes hours after activist Sonam Wangchuk announced he was ending his 26-day hunger strike. Representatives of India's Cockroach Janta Party (CJP) will hold talks with the government on Friday as thousands of people continue to protest day and night demanding the education minister's resignation. The meeting follows an eventful night where activist Sonam Wangchuk ended his hunger strike after 26 days of fasting in solidarity with the protests. Prime Minister Narendra Modi also posted a video promising a tough new law on examination paper leaks, the issue at the core of the protests.

stories.title: Almost €19m paid in prison staff allowances in 2025
stories.summary: Updated / Thursday, 23 Jul 2026 17:11 Getty The largest individual category was an operational allowance, which cost roughly €15. 7 million and was shared among 3,684 staff Almost €19 million was paid in allowances to prison staff last year, including extra payments for operating tuck shops, wearing plain clothes, handling dogs, and answering telephones. Irish Prison Service figures show €64,000 was paid to 23 staff under a "tuck shop" allowance in 2025. Another €79,000 was provided in plain-clothes allowances to 185 employees, while 78 staff shared just over €12,000 in telephone payments.

stories.title: macOS 27 public beta 2 arrives. Everything to know about the update.
stories.summary: macOS 27 public beta 2 arrives. Everything to know about the update. The second public beta for macOS 27 Golden Gate has just been released by Apple. The first beta brought some brand-new features to Mac, including a relaunch of Siri as a true AI assistant. Siri AI can answer questions based on a user's inbox, documents, and camera roll, and can also carry out tasks in a user's email, messages, and other apps. And, as Mashable previously covered, users can also access a hidden Siri AI interface, which is very much in its test phase, within the public beta. macOS 27 public beta 2 brings even more updates.

stories.title: Rare night heron spotted at National Trust site
stories.summary: The small bird, which is widespread across Africa, Asia, the Americas and Europe, is seen on camera. Rare night heron spotted at National Trust site Figure caption, By Clara Bullock Somerset A bird rarely seen in the UK has been caught on camera in Somerset, raising hopes that work to restore freshwater habitats could provide a refuge for it. The black-crowned night heron is widespread across Africa, Asia, the Americas and Europe but has now been spotted at National Trust's Holnicote Estate, near Minehead. This comes as conservationists at the estate introduced new freshwater habitats, providing a refuge for the heron, but also water voles, otters, pine martens, and even white-tailed eagles.

stories.title: End of an era as Worcester city centre bar announces permanent closure
stories.summary: LAST orders will be called at a long-standing bar in Worcester city centre after it was revealed it will permanently close. Slug & Lettuce will be bidding its customers farewell after over 15 years on The Cross because the business at the branch is "no longer commercially viable. " Owners of the chain, Stonegate, revealed it has been forced to make the difficult decision over significant challenges facing the hospitality sector. READ MORE: Final chapter for city restaurant as liquidators appointed The venue has called the historic St Nicholas Church opposite Subway its home for over 15 years after moving from the Cornmarket.

stories.title: Play area aims to interest children in rare habitat
stories.summary: A science centre hopes play is the key to making children care about the future of chalk streams. The future of one of England's most unique habitats could rest with play areas designed for children to interact with the rare environment, a science centre hopes. More than eight in 10 of the world's chalk streams are found in England, but the current prolonged hot and dry weather puts pressure on the wildlife that calls the rare waterways home. Winchester Science Centre, in Hampshire, has now created a play zone themed on the local landscape in the hope children will connect with the environments into adulthood.

stories.title: UK mortgage rates rise to highest level for a month as global tensions persist
stories.summary: "It will be incredibly frustrating for borrowers to see rates rise back up to where they were a month ago. The positive progress over recent weeks now feels all but lost, but what the market needs is a period of stability," said Rachel Springall, finance expert at Moneyfacts. She said 100 deals had been pulled temporarily as lenders reconsider their pricing plans. She suggested that anyone who needed to remortgage this year could lock in a new deal now with their existing lender ahead of time, but should also seek help from a broker to see whether there are better deals elsewhere.

stories.title: Urgent need for more security after physical attacks and abuse, say MPs’ staff
stories.summary: Staff employed by MPs are calling for urgently tightened security after reports of some being physically attacked and hundreds receiving threatening calls and messages. A survey of more than 500 MPs’ staff members found that 78% had received threatening communication and nearly one in three fear for their own safety or that of colleagues. Andy Burnham, the prime minister, is planning to announce a review of MPs’ security after the murder of Ann Widdecombe, a former Conservative minister and Reform UK spokesperson, this month. Joshua Kerry, 28, from Rotherham, South Yorkshire, has been charged with her murder .

stories.title: My generation shouldn’t be scared of a care tax – we’re paying one already | Gaby Hinsliff
stories.summary: One of the last things Andy Burnham did before entering Downing Street was to go and see his father . Now living in a nursing home, Roy Burnham has Alzheimer’s and isn’t really aware of his son’s new job. But perhaps the prime minister wanted, in part, to try to explain why he might not be visiting so often in future. He wouldn’t be the first middle-aged child torn between work pulling one way and elderly parents the other, ending up desperate for a politician willing (as Burnham Jr says he is) to “ expend quite a lot of political capital ” on the historically vote-losing task of fixing social care. For the alternative is families expending capital – financial and emotional – they don’t have.

stories.title: Replacing gas stove can help asthmatics as much as some drugs, US experts find
stories.summary: A US study has found a 70% drop in hospital admissions and days off from work or school when people with poorly controlled asthma had their gas stove and hob replaced with electric. Ohio’s publicly owned MetroHealth began the study in December 2024 but funding was terminated early by the incoming Trump administration. It had been designed to investigate if replacing gas cooking improved asthma for 1,200 people living near Cleveland, Ohio. Prof Ash Sehgal of Case Western Reserve University, the project’s leader, said: “We had completed the project at fewer than 100 households. As a result, over 1,100 households were unable to benefit. We also had to lay off about 20 project staff.

stories.title: Female veterans support group makes national impact
stories.summary: Chelley Tulloch, a former RAF medic, is one of 48 female veterans to benefit from the groups' help. Chelley Tulloch, a former RAF medic, has been working with the group and found it beneficial to her mental health. She experienced PTSD following multiple deployments to war zones around the world, where she helped injured soldiers who were being rescued from battles and taken to hospital. Tulloch said she was really desperate at times. "I just went through a lot of low moods, nightmares, seeing things, hearing things, smelling things, doubting myself, not being able to do things that I could do. "It really does affect the way your brain thinks.

stories.title: A Million-Panel Project
stories.summary: Utah’s new solar power and battery storage facility is among the largest in the region. 3 min read Image of the Day for July 24, 2026 Utah’s new solar power and battery storage facility is among the largest in the region. NASA Earth Observatory Jul 24, 2026 Article View more Images of the Day: Historically, central Utah’s Castle Valley has been a coal hub, with mining operations on the slopes of the Wasatch Plateau to the west active since the late 1800s. A different energy development arrived in the region in June 2026, when a large solar power and battery storage plant came online in the sunny valley about 130 miles (210 kilometers) southeast of Salt Lake City.

stories.title: ‘Sia: Nostalgic for the Present’ Review: A Concert Performance Film from 2016, but It Channels Sia’s Mystery and Power
stories.summary: Sia’s greatest songs, like "Unstoppable" and "Titanium" and "The Greatest," are anthems of strength, power, fortitude, invincibility, and watching the movie it’s fascinating to behold how her facelessness feeds that quality. She’s an abstract figure, a pair of lips singing, and this creates the sensation that the power she’s singing about doesn’t emanate from her. She’s channeling a force larger than herself — the rising specter of feminine triumph. She’s merely the vessel.

stories.title: Modi Caught Off Guard as India’s Economic Failings Inflame Young People
stories.summary: Prime Minister Narendra Modi was busy trying to put out other fires when a student movement suddenly exploded. There are no easy solutions to the problems behind it.

stories.title: Brent Crude Oil Prices Top $100 as Conflict With Iran Drags On
stories.summary: Stocks in Asia were down following a sell-off in the U.S. markets over soaring oil prices, rising borrowing costs and worries about the technology industry.

stories.title: Trump Administration Imposed New Tariffs of Around 10% on Over 80 Nations
stories.summary: The administration launched new duties tied to countries’ imports of goods made with forced labor, replacing a 10 percent tariff immediately after it expired.

stories.title: Trump’s Global Tariffs: What’s the Latest on the Trade War
stories.summary: The president has unveiled a complicated system to forge ahead with his trade war, after the Supreme Court stuck down his original levies.
entities: Supreme Court of the United States

stories.title: Indonesian babies were trafficked to Singapore. What happens to them now?
stories.summary: At least 12 babies have been trafficked from Indonesia to Singapore, but what will happen to them remains unclear. Indonesian babies were trafficked to Singapore. What happens to them now? Figure caption, Watch: A baby trafficking ring has been convicted - but what now for the babies? By Tessa Wong Reporting from Singapore and Astudestra Ajengrastri Reporting from Jakarta On Tuesday an Indonesian court convicted 19 people for baby trafficking, wrapping up a high-profile case - but leaving a key question unanswered. The members of the trafficking syndicate were found guilty of procuring at least 34 babies and selling them for adoption.

stories.title: Google fined €890m by EU for favouring its own apps over rivals
stories.summary: It is the first major enforcement action against the tech giant under the EU's new digital rules. Google has been fined €890m (£759m) by the EU for allegedly abusing its power by favouring its own apps and services over those from rivals. It is the first major action against the tech giant under the EU's Digital Markets Act (DMA), a landmark law aimed at curbing the power of the world's biggest tech companies. The Commission argued Google's practices limited consumer choice and gave its own services an unfair advantage over rivals. But the firm criticised the decision, arguing the EU's requirements could damage services used by millions of European customers.

stories.title: Judge Extends Pause for Paramount-Warner Bros. Deal
stories.summary: The court blocked the companies from closing the acquisition through mid-August, as a judge considers two lawsuits challenging it.

offbeat.title: Leeds United must be brave with record fee to seize rare James Trafford chance
offbeat.summary: For the second summer in succession, James Trafford has snubbed Newcastle United and, instead, is set to join Leeds United. Assuming the Yorkshire club recognise the opportunity before them. Right now, talks are on going between Leeds and Manchester City over the fee required to take the England goalkeeper over the Pennines. Leeds’ focus cannot be the cost – it must be the benefit. Because in Trafford, the Whites have the opportunity to lock in a No. 1 for seasons to come. Even if that doesn’t transpire, most likely, it will be because they have secured a hefty profit on whatever they invest in the 23-year-old.

offbeat.title: Aughinish Alumina investigation makes ‘no adverse findings’ against Co Limerick plant
offbeat.summary: An investigation into Aughinish Alumina and the company’s export of raw material to Russia has made “no adverse findings” against the Co Limerick plant, Minister for Enterprise Peter Burke has said. In a statement setting out the findings of the inquiry, Burke said there was “no information of a sufficient evidentiary level” found to confirm that alumina produced by the Russian-owned plant was later being used to supply Russian arms manufacturers making missiles and weapons for Moscow’s war in Ukraine.

offbeat.title: Italy pursue Pep Guardiola for national team job after Carlo Ancelotti rejection | Football News
offbeat.summary: The Italian football federation (FIGC) has held talks with Pep Guardiola and Carlo Ancelotti regarding the national team manager position, confirmed newly appointed technical director Paolo Maldini. Speaking at a news conference on Thursday, the former Azzurri defender and captain confirmed the federation’s ambitious shortlist. “Honestly, today we can't give news about what's happening,” Maldini told reporters when asked specifically about Guardiola. But he did confirm that the FIGC had also approached legendary Italian manager Ancelotti, currently in charge of the Brazil national team. “We can't hide that we also talked with Carlo Ancelotti before talking with Pep.

offbeat.title: 'Political will' can protect Aughinish jobs - Ukraine MP
offbeat.summary: 'Real political will' can protect Aughinish Alumina jobs, Ukrainian MP says Updated / Friday, 24 Jul 2026 07:44 Getty Ivanna Klympush-Tsintsadze is chair of the committee on Ukraine's integration with the EU The chair of the committee on Ukraine's integration with the EU has said there is a way to protect jobs at Aughinish Alumina if the company is sanctioned should the Irish Government have "the political will" to do so. An independent review into the Co Limerick refinery made no "adverse findings", however, Minister for Enterprise Peter Burke cited a "lack of verifiable internal trade data within Russia" since the country's invasion of Ukraine.

offbeat.title: Alejandro Garnacho makes Man Utd admission as he sets Aston Villa objective after Chelsea transfer
offbeat.summary: New Aston Villa signing Alejandro Garnacho believes Unai Emery can help him rediscover the form that first propelled him into the limelight at Manchester United. The 22-year-old winger has joined Villa on an initial season-long loan , with the move set to become permanent if he makes a specified number of appearances next season. Garnacho only joined Chelsea last summer in a £40 million move from Manchester United, but he struggled to produce his best form during his time at Stamford Bridge. Now embarking on a new chapter in his career, the Argentina international is eager to get started under Emery and make a fresh impact. “I’m really happy to be here,” he said on Villa TV.

offbeat.title: Southport killer Rudakubana transferred to Broadmoor hospital in Berkshire
offbeat.summary: Southport killer Axel Rudakubana has been transferred from prison to Broadmoor psychiatric hospital, the Ministry of Justice has confirmed. Broadmoor, a high-security psychiatric facility, is located in Crowthorne, just south of Bracknell. Officials approved Rudakubana's transfer on behalf of ministers, but a spokesperson for the department stressed it does not alter his 52-year sentence. Next Wednesday (July 29) marks two years since Rudakubana carried out an attack at a Taylor Swift-themed dance class in summer 2024, killing three girls. The move to Broadmoor high security hospital from the category A prison Belmarsh was first reported by the Guardian.

offbeat.title: Russian Su-57 fighter jet crashes in Moscow Oblast – photos
offbeat.summary: A Russian Su-57 fighter jet has crashed in Moscow Oblast. Source: Telegram channel Exilenova+, which publishes information on Russian equipment losses citing sources among Russian forces Quote: "More footage from the Odintsovo district, Moscow Oblast, where the Su-57 crashed. As for the pilot – we'll wait for official confirmation. There's none yet. " Details: Exilenova+ had earlier reported that, according to Russian sources, the pilot had managed to eject. "Update: the 'unrivalled' Su-57 was shot down by Moscow's air defence," Exilenova+ later added. The Telegram channel Taras Hryhorovych Reports also confirmed the aircraft's downing, specifying that it occurred during a training flight.

offbeat.title: The Game Takes a Bottle to the Head at 'VERZUZ' With YG
offbeat.summary: The Game temporarily turned a concert into a drinking contest during his VERZUZ event with YG , downing almost an entire bottle of tequila in a single gulp. During the event on Thursday (July 23), the Compton (or, as he repeatedly said it throughout the evening, “Bompton”) rapper took a giant swallow of what looked like Don Julio while onstage, and dropped the empty bottle to the ground in celebration when he was done. Don’t believe us? See it for yourself below. This VERZUZ matchup pits two generations of Compton against each other. The Game, born Jayceon Terrell Taylor, broke out as a Dr.

offbeat.title: Miquita Oliver apologises to Fearne Cotton over past jibe
offbeat.summary: Miquita Oliver apologises to Fearne Cotton over 'awful' comment in the past Updated / Wednesday, 22 Jul 2026 18:15 Getty (L-R) Miquita Oliver and Fearne Cotton - Oliver was let go from her radio job in 2005 following an interview with NME where she was asked how she would improve Top of the Pops and replied that the programme should get rid of Cotton Presenter Miquita Oliver has apologised to Fearne Cotton after Oliver was sacked from BBC Radio 1 in 2005 for making an "awful" comment about her fellow broadcaster.

offbeat.title: Trial opens for US mum who says postpartum issues led her to killing own children
offbeat.summary: Warning: This story contains distressing details and discussion of attempted suicide Jury selection has begun in Massachusetts in the case of a woman charged with killing all three of her children - whose defence team say she was suffering from postpartum psychosis. Prosecutors allege the killings carried out by Lindsay Clancy at her home in 2023 were premeditated and intentional. She has pleaded not guilty. Her legal team does not dispute that she carried out the killings, but is expected to argue that she was not responsible and raise an insanity defence. Clancy herself was also paralysed after attempting to take her own life the same night.

offbeat.title: Imperious Hegarty named The Sunday Game Hurler of Year
offbeat.summary: The Sunday Game have chosen Gearoid Hegarty as their Hurler of the Year with dominant All-Ireland champions Limerick taking up no less than 10 positions on the show's Team of the Year. Hegarty was at his imperious best as the Treaty County romped past Galway in Sunday's showpiece at Croke Park and he forms one-third of an all-Limerick half-forward line in our best 15 of the 2026 season. Beaten finalists Galway take up three spots on the final selection with one each for Clare and Cork. The Sunday Game Team of the Year 1. Nickie Quaid (Limerick) Another banner year for Limerick's last line of defence.

offbeat.title: ‘Up in smoke’: Attacks on ‘Russia’s Amazon’ bring Putin’s war closer to home
offbeat.summary: A series of Ukrainian strikes on the warehouses of Russia’s largest online retailer has sent potentially hundreds of millions of dollars in inventory up in flames, sparking an online furor among the country’s embattled small-business owners. Over the weekend, Ukrainian drones hit Wildberries distribution centers in Elektrostal in the Moscow region and Kotovsk in the Tambov region. A second wave of attacks early Wednesday hit logistics warehouses in the Krasnodar and Stavropol regions, while state media in the southwestern Voronezh region also reported that a drone strike forced the closure of a Wildberries warehouse there late Wednesday into Thursday.

offbeat.title: Wildfire emergency: France requests EU support as 23,000 evacuated in Biscarrosse wildfire crisis
offbeat.summary: Firefighting aircraft and helicopters from Portugal, Croatia, Czech Republic, and Slovakia are expected to arrive soon to aid French emergency crews. France has formally asked for assistance from other European Union countries as it battles devastating wildfires in the southwest of the country. President Emmanuel Macron confirmed that France has activated the European Union Civil Protection Mechanism, seeking urgent support, particularly for aerial firefighting. Aircraft and helicopters from Portugal, Croatia, the Czech Republic, and Slovakia are expected to join the efforts in the coming days. https://x. com/EmmanuelMacron/status/2080421021006405681?

offbeat.title: Chandrashekhar Azad: ‘This protest should have been Opposition’s job, not of students’
offbeat.summary: Azad Samaj Party (Kanshi Ram) chief and Nagina MP Chandrashekhar Azad was among the first politicians to engage with the Cockroach Janata Party (CJP)-led youth protests at Delhi’s Jantar Mantar. Azad speaks to The Indian Express on the reasons behind the protests, the July 20 violence, the government’s response, and the initial reluctance of Opposition parties to engage with it. Excerpts: * You were among the first politicians to associate with the CJP-led protests. Why did you think it was important when few other Opposition parties openly supported it?

offbeat.title: Minister declines to say whether Burnham will heed Lineker’s call for wealth tax
offbeat.summary: A Treasury minister has declined to say whether Andy Burnham’s Government will heed a call from millionaires including Gary Lineker for a higher tax on their wealth. Chief Secretary to the Treasury Emma Reynolds welcomed the offer, but said any announcement on tax policy would be made in the budget. Sports pundit and former footballer Lineker is among 120 UK-based millionaires to sign an open letter to the Prime Minister saying they should pay more tax. The “Proud to Pay” letter published on Thursday claims the money raised from higher taxes on wealth could be used to reduce inequality, support public infrastructure, and back small businesses.

offbeat.title: UK HMRC Plans 30,000 Retail Enforcement Actions as Illegal Tobacco and Vape Sellers Face Tougher Crackdown
offbeat.summary: The UK government has announced that HM Revenue & Customs (HMRC) will carry out more than 30,000 interventions targeting businesses and retail premises during the 2026-2027 financial year. The actions will focus on tax fraud, illegal goods sales and businesses involved in unlawful activities through retail channels. The government said illegal tobacco and illegal vape sales remain areas of concern. The move shows that UK enforcement against illegal nicotine products is expanding from import and supply channels toward retail-level oversight, alongside the upcoming introduction of the Vaping Products Duty. Key Points HMRC plans more than 30,000 retail interventions in 2026-2027.

offbeat.title: The transfer market, coaching staff, and management are targeting a 22-man squad. Bologna also includes Amondarain. Freuler's departure opens another door.
offbeat.summary: The transfer market, coaching staff, and management are targeting a 22-man squad. Bologna also includes Amondarain. Freuler's departure opens another door. Change of plans. "I love small squads, where everyone can compete for a spot and be a key player. Without Europe, we don't need too much rotation. I think a squad of 18-20 players will be enough," Domenico Tedesco stated on the day of his presentation. Meanwhile, the manager can be pleased with the arrival of Mikel Amondarain, announced last night, who was one of the main midfield targets. Ten million plus two million in bonuses would snatch the player from English clubs who had been pursuing him for some time.

offbeat.title: Zapatero Insists on Innocence Amid Scrutiny Over Jewelry Origins
offbeat.summary: Former Prime Minister José Luis Rodríguez Zapatero insists on his innocence, stating he received the €1.3 million jewelry as a long-standing courtesy gift with no personal financial motive, reigniting discussions about political responsibility and transparency in Spain.

offbeat.title: Morning newspapers can already see Míchel’s hand in winning Ajax
offbeat.summary: Pro Shots Jul 23, 2026 22:55-07:00 Translated by Ajax drew praise after their 1-4 win over FK Vojvodina in the second qualifying round of the Conference League. A day later, the Dutch newspapers agreed that coach Míchel's influence is becoming increasingly visible. According to De Telegraaf , Ajax played for an hour exactly as the Spanish coach wanted. There are, however, still areas to improve. ''Attacking, at times flamboyant and technically polished. The fact that Ajax also missed a great many chances and faded badly in the final half-hour confirmed his words just before the match. '' Míchel had said it would take time before the players fully took on board his way of playing.

offbeat.title: Maya 2027.2 trains MotionMaker
offbeat.summary: Table of Contents Show For those who don’t know the tool: Autodesk Maya is a DCC for modeling, rigging, animation, FX, USD and rendering. MotionMaker sits in the Animation Editor, feeding previs, crowds and character blocking before rigs, layers, retargeting and Arnold renders take over. Maya gets the AI work where artists live The current Maya update is more interesting as a DCC update than as another AI press-cycle snack. The centre of gravity is MotionMaker, the AI-assisted animation system now moving from supplied motion models toward user-trained character models.

offbeat.title: Woman who lost husband to suicide after he was admitted to CUH says change is needed
offbeat.summary: Nancy Reilly said they were “crazy happy” but their idyllic life was shattered when Mark took his own life after being admitted to a single room in .

offbeat.title: Walla Walla Iris Society Annual Rhizome Sale
offbeat.summary: WALLA - Rhonda and Charles Nosler of Waitsburg have been members of the Walla Iris Society since 2019 and currently have over 150 iris varieties. The WWIS will hold its annual sale at the Milton-Freewater Community Building on August 1 and 2 from 9 a. m. to 5 p. m. Irises will be presented by name, with variety information and a photo. For newcomers to growing irises, planting and care instructions will be available. The sale will include tall, intermediate, border, miniature tall, and standard dwarf varieties of bearded irises. Bearded irises are perennials that grow well in the Walla Valley, with its hot summers, cold winters, and well-drained soil.

offbeat.title: Peter Phillips and Harriet Sperling are joined by her teenage daughter, Georgina, to support Prince William at a charity polo day
offbeat.summary: From their first meeting to their wedding day, family has been at the heart of Peter Phillips and Harriet Sperling's royal romance. So it is of no surprise their three daughters continue to play such an important part in their lives now that they're married. Last week, Harriet and Peter were joined by her daughter, Georgina, as they made a low-key appearance at the DMMI Royal Charity Polo Cup at Guards Polo Club, Windsor.

offbeat.title: What is China’s Kimi K3 and why is the US so rattled by it?
offbeat.summary: What is China’s Kimi K3 and why is the US so rattled by it? Remember DeepSeek? The Chinese artificial intelligence startup that rattled the US tech industry with its model that nearly matched leading rivals, trained at a fraction of the cost? China may have done it again. The latest Chinese AI model making waves was developed by a lesser-known startup called Moonshot AI. In just two days after launch, Moonshot suspended new subscriptions after demand overwhelmed its computing capacity.

offbeat.title: Almost €19m paid in prison staff allowances in 2025
offbeat.summary: Updated / Thursday, 23 Jul 2026 17:11 Getty The largest individual category was an operational allowance, which cost roughly €15. 7 million and was shared among 3,684 staff Almost €19 million was paid in allowances to prison staff last year, including extra payments for operating tuck shops, wearing plain clothes, handling dogs, and answering telephones. Irish Prison Service figures show €64,000 was paid to 23 staff under a "tuck shop" allowance in 2025. Another €79,000 was provided in plain-clothes allowances to 185 employees, while 78 staff shared just over €12,000 in telephone payments.

offbeat.title: macOS 27 public beta 2 arrives. Everything to know about the update.
offbeat.summary: macOS 27 public beta 2 arrives. Everything to know about the update. The second public beta for macOS 27 Golden Gate has just been released by Apple. The first beta brought some brand-new features to Mac, including a relaunch of Siri as a true AI assistant. Siri AI can answer questions based on a user's inbox, documents, and camera roll, and can also carry out tasks in a user's email, messages, and other apps. And, as Mashable previously covered, users can also access a hidden Siri AI interface, which is very much in its test phase, within the public beta. macOS 27 public beta 2 brings even more updates.

offbeat.title: End of an era as Worcester city centre bar announces permanent closure
offbeat.summary: LAST orders will be called at a long-standing bar in Worcester city centre after it was revealed it will permanently close. Slug & Lettuce will be bidding its customers farewell after over 15 years on The Cross because the business at the branch is "no longer commercially viable. " Owners of the chain, Stonegate, revealed it has been forced to make the difficult decision over significant challenges facing the hospitality sector. READ MORE: Final chapter for city restaurant as liquidators appointed The venue has called the historic St Nicholas Church opposite Subway its home for over 15 years after moving from the Cornmarket.

offbeat.title: UK mortgage rates rise to highest level for a month as global tensions persist
offbeat.summary: "It will be incredibly frustrating for borrowers to see rates rise back up to where they were a month ago. The positive progress over recent weeks now feels all but lost, but what the market needs is a period of stability," said Rachel Springall, finance expert at Moneyfacts. She said 100 deals had been pulled temporarily as lenders reconsider their pricing plans. She suggested that anyone who needed to remortgage this year could lock in a new deal now with their existing lender ahead of time, but should also seek help from a broker to see whether there are better deals elsewhere.

offbeat.title: Indonesian babies were trafficked to Singapore. What happens to them now?
offbeat.summary: At least 12 babies have been trafficked from Indonesia to Singapore, but what will happen to them remains unclear. Indonesian babies were trafficked to Singapore. What happens to them now? Figure caption, Watch: A baby trafficking ring has been convicted - but what now for the babies? By Tessa Wong Reporting from Singapore and Astudestra Ajengrastri Reporting from Jakarta On Tuesday an Indonesian court convicted 19 people for baby trafficking, wrapping up a high-profile case - but leaving a key question unanswered. The members of the trafficking syndicate were found guilty of procuring at least 34 babies and selling them for adoption.

health.title: One year, 17 towns, dozens of voices: what we heard along the coast of England and Wales
health.summary: I f you were asked to summon up images of the coastline of England and Wales, you might think of sunny days, buckets, spades and carefree holidays. But for many young people growing up by the sea, decades of evidence tell a very different story. For them, rather than a picture-perfect horizon full of hope, the sea has become a boundary that swallows up opportunity. With a 73% decline in funding for youth services in England over the past 12 years, and a lack of opportunities that has led to nearly 1 million 16- to 24-year-oldsnot in work, education or training (Neet), the situation for young people, especially in places of deprivation, has never seemed more desperate.

health.title: Replacing gas stove can help asthmatics as much as some drugs, US experts find
health.summary: A US study has found a 70% drop in hospital admissions and days off from work or school when people with poorly controlled asthma had their gas stove and hob replaced with electric. Ohio’s publicly owned MetroHealth began the study in December 2024 but funding was terminated early by the incoming Trump administration. It had been designed to investigate if replacing gas cooking improved asthma for 1,200 people living near Cleveland, Ohio. Prof Ash Sehgal of Case Western Reserve University, the project’s leader, said: “We had completed the project at fewer than 100 households. As a result, over 1,100 households were unable to benefit. We also had to lay off about 20 project staff.

health.title: Female veterans support group makes national impact
health.summary: Chelley Tulloch, a former RAF medic, is one of 48 female veterans to benefit from the groups' help. Chelley Tulloch, a former RAF medic, has been working with the group and found it beneficial to her mental health. She experienced PTSD following multiple deployments to war zones around the world, where she helped injured soldiers who were being rescued from battles and taken to hospital. Tulloch said she was really desperate at times. "I just went through a lot of low moods, nightmares, seeing things, hearing things, smelling things, doubting myself, not being able to do things that I could do. "It really does affect the way your brain thinks.

politics.title: ‘A small snippet’: what pubs make of Burnham’s £100m rates giveaway
politics.summary: Landlords say high costs of running hospitality business outweigh potential savings of business rates discount One of Andy Burnham’s first policy measures as prime minister is a 20% reduction in business rates for pubs, clubs and live music venues. The policy, announced on Thursday, is part of a broader agenda to support the high street and will cost £100m. Continue reading. One of Andy Burnham’s first policy measures as prime minister is a 20% reduction in business rates for pubs, clubs and live music venues. So how does the policy measure up in the context of running a business and where does the money on your bar bill actually go?

politics.title: Urgent need for more security after physical attacks and abuse, say MPs’ staff
politics.summary: Staff employed by MPs are calling for urgently tightened security after reports of some being physically attacked and hundreds receiving threatening calls and messages. A survey of more than 500 MPs’ staff members found that 78% had received threatening communication and nearly one in three fear for their own safety or that of colleagues. Andy Burnham, the prime minister, is planning to announce a review of MPs’ security after the murder of Ann Widdecombe, a former Conservative minister and Reform UK spokesperson, this month. Joshua Kerry, 28, from Rotherham, South Yorkshire, has been charged with her murder .

politics.title: My generation shouldn’t be scared of a care tax – we’re paying one already | Gaby Hinsliff
politics.summary: One of the last things Andy Burnham did before entering Downing Street was to go and see his father . Now living in a nursing home, Roy Burnham has Alzheimer’s and isn’t really aware of his son’s new job. But perhaps the prime minister wanted, in part, to try to explain why he might not be visiting so often in future. He wouldn’t be the first middle-aged child torn between work pulling one way and elderly parents the other, ending up desperate for a politician willing (as Burnham Jr says he is) to “ expend quite a lot of political capital ” on the historically vote-losing task of fixing social care. For the alternative is families expending capital – financial and emotional – they don’t have.

breaking.title: US hits dozens of trading partners with new wave of tariffs
breaking.summary: Imposed citing forced labour concerns, the levies replace a temporary global duty brought in after the US Supreme Court struck down tariffs in February. The US has imposed new tariffs on 60 trading partners as a temporary global levy brought in after the Supreme Court ruled against the Trump administration in February expires. The duties, ranging from 10% to 12. 5% and accounting for the vast majority of American imports, target key economic partners over claims they have failed to properly tackle forced labour – including the UK, China, the European Union, Canada, Japan and India.

breaking.title: India's 'cockroach' protesters and government to hold talks as stalemate continues
breaking.summary: The meeting comes hours after activist Sonam Wangchuk announced he was ending his 26-day hunger strike. Representatives of India's Cockroach Janta Party (CJP) will hold talks with the government on Friday as thousands of people continue to protest day and night demanding the education minister's resignation. The meeting follows an eventful night where activist Sonam Wangchuk ended his hunger strike after 26 days of fasting in solidarity with the protests. Prime Minister Narendra Modi also posted a video promising a tough new law on examination paper leaks, the issue at the core of the protests.

breaking.title: Modi Caught Off Guard as India’s Economic Failings Inflame Young People
breaking.summary: Prime Minister Narendra Modi was busy trying to put out other fires when a student movement suddenly exploded. There are no easy solutions to the problems behind it.

sports.title: Socceroos star allegedly tests positive for cocaine after speeding
sports.summary: Socceroos World Cup star Cristian Volpato allegedly tests positive for cocaine after being twice caught for speeding in Sydney. Socceroos World Cup star Cristian Volpato has allegedly tested positive for cocaine after police twice stopped him for speeding in Sydney on the same night. The 22-year-old Australia midfielder was stopped in a BMW Coupe on the Anzac Bridge at 11:05pm local time on Thursday night after he was caught travelling between 86-94km/h in a 60km/h zone, police said. He was subjected to a roadside breath test, which returned a negative result.

facts.title: Rare night heron spotted at National Trust site
facts.summary: The small bird, which is widespread across Africa, Asia, the Americas and Europe, is seen on camera. Rare night heron spotted at National Trust site Figure caption, By Clara Bullock Somerset A bird rarely seen in the UK has been caught on camera in Somerset, raising hopes that work to restore freshwater habitats could provide a refuge for it. The black-crowned night heron is widespread across Africa, Asia, the Americas and Europe but has now been spotted at National Trust's Holnicote Estate, near Minehead. This comes as conservationists at the estate introduced new freshwater habitats, providing a refuge for the heron, but also water voles, otters, pine martens, and even white-tailed eagles.

facts.title: Play area aims to interest children in rare habitat
facts.summary: A science centre hopes play is the key to making children care about the future of chalk streams. The future of one of England's most unique habitats could rest with play areas designed for children to interact with the rare environment, a science centre hopes. More than eight in 10 of the world's chalk streams are found in England, but the current prolonged hot and dry weather puts pressure on the wildlife that calls the rare waterways home. Winchester Science Centre, in Hampshire, has now created a play zone themed on the local landscape in the hope children will connect with the environments into adulthood.

facts.title: A Million-Panel Project
facts.summary: Utah’s new solar power and battery storage facility is among the largest in the region. 3 min read Image of the Day for July 24, 2026 Utah’s new solar power and battery storage facility is among the largest in the region. NASA Earth Observatory Jul 24, 2026 Article View more Images of the Day: Historically, central Utah’s Castle Valley has been a coal hub, with mining operations on the slopes of the Wasatch Plateau to the west active since the late 1800s. A different energy development arrived in the region in June 2026, when a large solar power and battery storage plant came online in the sunny valley about 130 miles (210 kilometers) southeast of Salt Lake City.

entertainment.title: ‘Sia: Nostalgic for the Present’ Review: A Concert Performance Film from 2016, but It Channels Sia’s Mystery and Power
entertainment.summary: Sia’s greatest songs, like "Unstoppable" and "Titanium" and "The Greatest," are anthems of strength, power, fortitude, invincibility, and watching the movie it’s fascinating to behold how her facelessness feeds that quality. She’s an abstract figure, a pair of lips singing, and this creates the sensation that the power she’s singing about doesn’t emanate from her. She’s channeling a force larger than herself — the rising specter of feminine triumph. She’s merely the vessel.

tech.title: Brent Crude Oil Prices Top $100 as Conflict With Iran Drags On
tech.summary: Stocks in Asia were down following a sell-off in the U.S. markets over soaring oil prices, rising borrowing costs and worries about the technology industry.

tech.title: Google fined €890m by EU for favouring its own apps over rivals
tech.summary: It is the first major enforcement action against the tech giant under the EU's new digital rules. Google has been fined €890m (£759m) by the EU for allegedly abusing its power by favouring its own apps and services over those from rivals. It is the first major action against the tech giant under the EU's Digital Markets Act (DMA), a landmark law aimed at curbing the power of the world's biggest tech companies. The Commission argued Google's practices limited consumer choice and gave its own services an unfair advantage over rivals. But the firm criticised the decision, arguing the EU's requirements could damage services used by millions of European customers.

tech.title: Judge Extends Pause for Paramount-Warner Bros. Deal
tech.summary: The court blocked the companies from closing the acquisition through mid-August, as a judge considers two lawsuits challenging it.

crisis.title: Trump Administration Imposed New Tariffs of Around 10% on Over 80 Nations
crisis.summary: The administration launched new duties tied to countries’ imports of goods made with forced labor, replacing a 10 percent tariff immediately after it expired.

crisis.title: Trump’s Global Tariffs: What’s the Latest on the Trade War
crisis.summary: The president has unveiled a complicated system to forge ahead with his trade war, after the Supreme Court stuck down his original levies.
entities: Supreme Court of the United States
